text:
Once upon a time in a small town nestled among rolling hills, there lived a young bird named Willow. Willow loved to explore every corner of her enchanted woodland home.

One particular morning, as the sun peeked through the leaves, casting dappled patterns on the ground, Willow gathered her fluttering friends to attend the annual Harmony Festival. Birds of various plumages flew down from the treetops to join her.

Throughout the day, they sang sweet songs. Willow joined in, her song a cheerful hum.

As the sun began to dip lower in the sky, casting amber shadows through the trees, they settled down to listen to some famous tales told by the old owl.

The first story was about a daring squirrel who'd learned magic. She could make anything disappear!

Willow fluttered in awe.

The second was about a wise old owl who lived in a mountain cave. Every night she conjured up delicious berries.

Willow swooped closer to hear that.
